summary: Split the tail of the fighter unit ft_081B into a new linked, matching unit ft_084E. The PR moved functions from ft_80084E1C through ft_80085204 into src/melee/ft/ft_084E.c, added ft_084E.h prototypes, updated splits and symbols for the new text/sdata2 ranges, and changed configure.py so both ft_081B.c and ft_084E.c build as Matching objects. The decomp.dev report showed +14052 bytes linked code and 16 new matches, with the apparent broken ft_081B matches corresponding to functions intentionally relocated into ft_084E.

key_files.role: New split source file containing the moved matching fighter routines ft_80084E1C through ft_80085204.
Original source file shortened to end at ft_80084DB0 after removing the relocated 084E-0852 functions.
New header declaring the relocated ft_084E function prototypes.
Removed relocated prototypes and temporarily includes ft_084E.h as a transitive include hack.
Adjusted ft_081B text/sdata2 end addresses and introduced the ft_084E split range.
Updated local sdata2 symbols around 0x804D83A8-0x804D83B8 from ft_* names to compiler-style @ labels.
Changed ft_081B from NonMatching to Matching and added ft_084E as a Matching object.

what_changed: Removed those same routines from ft_081B.c so ft_081B.c now covers text 0x80081B38-0x80084E1C instead of 0x80081B38-0x8008521C.
Added splits.txt entry for melee/ft/ft_084E.c with text 0x80084E1C-0x8008521C and sdata2 0x804D83B8-0x804D83C0.
Narrowed ft_081B.c sdata2 range from 0x804D83A8-0x804D83C0 to 0x804D83A8-0x804D83B8.
Reclassified ft_081B.c as Matching in configure.py and added ft_084E.c as a Matching object.
Moved prototypes for the relocated functions into new ft_084E.h.
Left a TODO in ft_081B.h noting that including ft_084E.h there is a transitive include hack and direct dependers should include ft_084E.h themselves.
smart_moves: Performed a clean range split at address 0x80084E1C, preserving function ordering and matching the new source filename to the split boundary.
Updated source, header, splits, symbols, and configure.py together, which is required for a split to become linked code rather than just copied source.
Kept the relocated code essentially intact, minimizing behavioral or matching risk while moving it into the new object.
Used a temporary transitive include in ft_081B.h to avoid breaking existing dependers immediately, while documenting that this should eventually be replaced by direct includes.
Converted both affected units to Matching only after the split boundaries and local sdata2 ownership were aligned.
decomp_lessons: When splitting an already partially decompiled file, adjust both .text and .sdata2 ranges in splits.txt; local float constants may need to move with the new object boundary.

A decomp.dev report may show relocated functions as broken matches in the old unit and new matches in the new unit; interpret this as expected during file splits if the functions reappear at 100% in the new unit.
After moving functions into a new split unit, move their declarations into a new header named for that unit and remove stale declarations from the old header.
If existing downstream files still include the old header, a transitive include can be used as a temporary migration tactic, but it should be marked as a hack and later replaced with direct includes.
Build configuration must reflect the new object list and matching status; otherwise a correct source split will not link as intended.
naming_conventions: New split source/header followed the address-based fighter unit naming pattern: ft_084E.c and ft_084E.h for functions beginning at ft_80084E1C.
Function names remained address-based ft_80084E1C through ft_80085204; no semantic renaming was attempted in this PR.
Header guard used the project-style path-based macro MELEE_FT_084E_H.
Local sdata2 symbol names were changed from ft_804D83A8-style address labels to compiler-style local labels such as @195, @333, @1111, @1153, and @204.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split object ranges exactly at function boundary 0x80084E1C and next unit boundary 0x8008521C.
Preserved stack-padding-sensitive code such as PAD_STACK(8) in ft_80084F3C during the move.
Retained float literal spelling with 0.0F and existing f32/float local typing to avoid perturbing codegen.
Maintained direct field access patterns such as gobj->user_data in ft_80085030 and ft_800850E0 rather than normalizing everything to GET_FIGHTER, likely preserving matching behavior.
Included <math.h> in ft_084E.c for cosf and sinf used by ft_80085154.
Configured the split units as Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py after matching was confirmed.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 linked code increased to 38.75%, +0.36% and +14052 bytes; matched data stayed effectively unchanged while linked data gained +24 bytes.
Reported 16 new matches, including 14 functions in main/melee/ft/ft_084E, ft_084E .sdata2, and ft_081B .sdata2 reaching 100%.
Reported 14 broken matches in main/melee/ft/ft_081B for the moved functions; context indicates these are relocations into ft_084E rather than regressions.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove the transitive include hack from src/melee/ft/ft_081B.h.
Search for direct dependers of ft_80084E1C through ft_80085204 that should include ft_084E.h directly.
Compare nearby split PRs for ft_0819.c and ft_0852.c to see if the same split-and-link pattern was reused.
Audit local sdata2 labels around 0x804D83A8-0x804D83C0 for ownership after adjacent fighter splits.
